sections.dashboardDescription: Workspace overview, quick stats, and discovered local items.
sections.diagnosticsDescription: System checks, release cache, and activity logs.
sections.editorsDescription: Installed versions, paths, and default editor.
sections.projectsDescription: Local library, project creation, and quick launch.
sections.releasesDescription: Official downloads from the Godot repository.
sections.settingsDescription: Directories used by the hub for installations and projects.

dashboard.scanPendingBody: Run a workspace scan to find local editors and projects that are not registered yet.

onboarding.body: Install an official Godot version, set default paths, and keep local projects ready to open like Unity Hub, Epic Launcher, and Unreal Project Browser.
onboarding.engineInstallBody: Choose stable or prerelease builds from Godot GitHub.
onboarding.projectLibraryBody: Creation, import, favorites, and version-based launch.
onboarding.slideOneBody: Keep projects, engine versions, and Git workflow in one focused desktop hub.
onboarding.slideOneTitle: A cleaner launchpad for Godot work.
onboarding.slideThreeBody: Point Forge at official Godot releases or trusted forks, then install the right build for this machine.
onboarding.slideThreeTitle: Use the Godot ecosystem you trust.
onboarding.slideTwoBody: Create, import, favorite, and open local projects without hunting through folders.
onboarding.title: Your Godot launcher with a studio workflow.

projects.createNewOrImport: Create a new project or import a folder with project.godot.
projects.editorRequired: Install a Godot editor before creating or editing projects.
projects.noEditorCtaBody: Install a Godot editor before creating, importing, or launching projects.
projects.noEditorCtaTitle: Install an engine to launch projects

projectPage.dangerBody: Removing only removes the project from the library; it does not delete the folder.
projectPage.selectProject: Select a project to view details and actions.
projectPage.selectProjectGit: Select a project to check Git.

editors.noEngineBody: Install an engine from Releases to start opening projects.

releases.adjustFilters: Clear or adjust filters to show more releases.
releases.fetchOfficial: Fetch releases and install the right build for your system.
releases.filterPanelHint: Refine the release catalog without crowding the toolbar.
releases.title: Install Godot versions

settings.control: Control where Forge installs engines and creates projects.
settings.confirmRestoreBody: Restore default paths, appearance preferences, and release sources? This will not delete projects or installed editors.
settings.githubTokenHint: Optional. Used only for GitHub release requests to avoid public API rate limits. Environment variables GITHUB_TOKEN or GH_TOKEN take priority.
settings.githubTokenStepOne: Open GitHub token settings and create a fine-grained personal access token.
settings.githubTokenStepTwo: For public release downloads, avoid extra repository permissions unless you need private repositories.
settings.githubTokenStepThree: Copy the token once, paste it here, then save workspace settings.
settings.licensesBody: Source code, brand assets, and attribution notices are governed by separate repository documents.
settings.migrateExistingPaths: Move registered items when paths change
settings.migrateExistingPathsHint: When saving new default folders, move editors and projects currently inside the old default folders to the new folders. Items registered outside those folders stay where they are.
settings.noDiscoveredEditors: No editors found in the configured installation folder.
settings.noDiscoveredProjects: No projects found in the configured project folder.
settings.releaseRepositoryHint: Godot official releases are always included. Extra sources currently support GitHub owner/name, HTTPS URLs, or SSH URLs.
settings.workspaceScanBody: Checks the configured Godot installations and project folders, flags broken entries, and lets you add valid items to Forge.
settings.systemReadinessBody: Quick checks for Git, workspace folders, GitHub API limits, and release cache health.

security.body: Godot Forge never needs your GitHub password, Godot credentials, recovery codes, or private keys.
security.ruleCredentials: Maintainers and support channels should never ask for passwords, MFA codes, wallet keys, SSH private keys, or recovery phrases.
security.ruleLocal: Editor paths, project paths, and Git operations are local to your machine unless you explicitly push through Git.
security.ruleReporting: Treat unexpected requests for credentials or builds as suspicious and report them through the official security process.
security.title: Security policy

confirmDelete.editorBody: Remove "{name}" from the editor list? This will not delete files from disk.
confirmDelete.projectBody: Remove "{name}" from the library? This will not delete the project folder.
confirmDelete.title: Confirm removal
